title: Config-schema evolution has no load-time migration path
description: loadConfigForDir parses config.json then calls validateFromFile(bundledSchemaPath("config"), …) and returns — no schema_version check, no migration step. A schema-name-keyed migration mechanism exists (validateBlockWithMigrationForDir: compare schema.version to data.schema_version, walk the registered chain on mismatch, then validate) and "config" is a valid schemaName, but config-load never invokes it. When config.schema.json advances to a shape the stored config does not satisfy, every config-loading op fails validation (exit 5) with no path to carry the config across the version gap. The class: read-time validation and migration require a versioned-data envelope; config carries no schema_version, so the mechanism is uninvoked and config's raw-validate brick is the acute symptom.

evidence.reference: loadConfigForDir parses config.json then validateFromFile(bundledSchemaPath("config"), data, …) and returns — no schema_version check, no migration call

validateBlockWithMigrationForDir: block-data load reads schema.version vs the item's schema_version, runs runMigrations over the registered chain on mismatch, then validates — the mechanism config-load lacks
the migration registry is keyed by schemaName; resolve()/runMigrations would walk a "config" chain identically to a block name; config-load simply never calls it

impact: A breaking config-schema bump strands the live substrate: config.json fails validation against the rebuilt CLI and every config-loading op exits 5, with no sanctioned recovery (amend-config also validates-before-mutate). Reproduced in this project: the TASK-020 state_derivation.blocked_by shape change bricked the live .context until the change was reverted. Until a config-migration path exists, every future config-schema shape change carries the same live-substrate-bricking risk.
canonical_vocabulary: schema migration (forward migration / version-gated transform applied at load)
proposed_resolution: RESOLVED at TASK-070: loadConfigForDir migrates-then-validates via the schema-name-keyed config chain (fail-fast on unresolvable chain, no raw-validate fallback); map_each declarative-transform primitive shipped (table + set-on-each modes); catalog identity config 1.0.0→1.7.0 declaration in samples/migrations.json with idempotent seeding at every ceremony entry point (write ceremonies materialize-then-seed; read ceremonies no-op on a nonexistent dir); the live substrate's config chain filed via write-schema-migration. Verified VER-060. --- Original: Add a load-time config-migration path mirroring validateBlockWithMigrationForDir: loadConfigForDir reads the config's schema_version and, on mismatch with the bundled schema version, walks the schema-name-keyed migration chain for "config" before validating. Requires a declarative-transform primitive able to map array elements (map_each) and a config migration registry entry. Verified design + corrected wiring: analysis/2026-06-18-config-migration-corrected-implementation-steps.md (and the same-date feasibility/validation md's). File at the class level (schema-evolution safety: read-time validation + migration require a versioned-data envelope).
